---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 7: Model Interpretation for Scientific Insight
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Model Interpretation for Scientific Insight
- Unit role in MG pipeline.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Interpret models without overclaiming causality.

## 03. Recap from Unit 6
- Uncertainty-aware prediction context.

## 04. Why interpretation matters in materials science
- From prediction to understanding.

## 05. Interpretability taxonomy
- Global vs local explanations.

## 06. Intrinsic vs post-hoc interpretability
- Tradeoffs and use cases.

## 07. Linear model coefficients
- Strengths and caveats.

## 08. Tree-based feature importance
- Split-based intuition.

## 09. Permutation importance
- Model-agnostic signal.

## 10. SHAP intuition
- Additive contribution framing.

## 11. Partial dependence plots
- Marginal effect visualization.

## 12. ICE plots
- Heterogeneous local behaviors.

## 13. Counterfactual explanations
- Minimal-change insights.

## 14. Interaction effects
- Non-additive behavior detection.

## 15. Correlated feature pitfalls
- Attribution instability.

## 16. Data leakage and fake importance
- Spurious explanation risks.

## 17. Uncertainty in explanations
- Confidence on interpretation.

## 18. Stability analysis for explanations
- Across folds/seeds.

## 19. Domain priors in interpretation
- Physics-informed plausibility checks.

## 20. Causal caution
- Association is not mechanism.

## 21. Scientific hypothesis generation
- Interpretation as proposal engine.

## 22. Triangulation with experiments
- Closing the loop.

## 23. Multi-fidelity interpretation issues
- Source-dependent effects.

## 24. Subgroup interpretation
- Chemistry/structure slices.

## 25. OOD behavior interpretation
- When explanations break.

## 26. Visualization best practices
- Clear, non-misleading plots.

## 27. Reporting standards
- What to include in papers.

## 28. Model cards for explainability
- Documentation checklist.

## 29. Case sketch: bandgap model insights
- End-to-end interpretation scaffold.

## 30. Case sketch: stability classifier
- Decision logic analysis.

## 31. Case sketch: synthesis-property links
- Feature interaction exploration.

## 32. Failure mode #1
- Confident but unstable explanations.

## 33. Failure mode #2
- Explanation drift under retraining.

## 34. Failure mode #3
- Domain-inconsistent attributions.

## 35. Mitigation checklist
- Robust interpretation workflow.

## 36. Human expert review loop
- Joint interpretation process.

## 37. Interpretation for actionability
- What can be changed in lab.

## 38. Ethics and responsible communication
- Avoid overclaiming certainty.

## 39. Bridge to inverse design
- Interpretation to optimization.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: baseline interpretation
- Feature importance + PDP/ICE.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: SHAP analysis
- Local and global views.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: stability check
- Repeat across random seeds.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: domain sanity test
- Physics-consistency evaluation.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: report writing
- Communicate insights + caveats.

## 45. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 46. Summary table
- Methods vs strengths/limitations.

## 47. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 48. Reading assignment
- Interpretability references.

## 49. Transition preview
- Unit 8 inverse design.

## 50. References
- Source list.

::: {#refs}
:::
